question.text: Following SEBI's restriction on weekly options, which of the following is TRUE about the current weekly options landscape in India?

answer.text: NSE and BSE can each offer weekly expiry options on their respective single benchmark index only
Only NSE is allowed to offer weekly options, BSE cannot
Weekly options are now available on all stock options as well
Both NSE and BSE must use the same benchmark index for weekly options

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under the SEBI circular: - Each exchange INDEPENDENTLY chooses its one benchmark index for weekly options - NSE chose Nifty 50 (or Bank Nifty as applicable) - BSE chose Sensex (or Bankex as applicable) - Both exchanges can have weekly options, but only on ONE index each
**The Trap:** Both exchanges can offer weekly options - the restriction is on the NUMBER of indices per exchange, not on which exchanges can offer them.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This replaced the previous system where multiple indices had weekly options.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Each exchange = one benchmark index for weekly expiry. NSE and BSE choose independently.
